topic: Variables and Expressions / Order of Operations

questions.question: Translate the phrase '5 more than 3 times a number' into an algebraic expression.
A bookstore is having a sale where everything is 15% off. If a book originally costs $25, how much will it cost during the sale?
A person wants to buy a new bike that costs $120. If they have a $20 coupon and 10% off, how much will they pay?

A coffee shop has a promotion where customers can buy a large coffee for $2.50 and get a small coffee for $1.50. If a customer buys 2 large coffees and 2 small coffees, how much will they pay in total?

A person is planning a road trip that is 240 miles long. If they drive at an average speed of 40 miles per hour, how many hours will the trip take? Use the expression $time = \frac{distance}{speed}$ to solve the problem. Show all work and calculations.

A water tank can hold 1200 gallons of water. If 300 gallons of water are already in the tank, and 50 gallons of water are added every hour, how many hours will it take to fill the tank? Use the expression $total\ water = initial\ water + (rate \cdot time)$ to solve the problem. Show all work and calculations.

teacher_rules: Make sure students have a calculator
Encourage students to read the questions carefully
Remind students to show all work and calculations for FRQs
